page: Vultr voc-s-1c-8gb-150s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring a single shared AMD EPYC vCPU operating at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. This single-core, single-threaded configuration is paired with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB per core resource density. Local storage is provided via a bundled 150 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. The CPU architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 vector extensions (AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16) alongside hardware-accelerated cryptography features like AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

Performance benchmarks reveal a stark contrast between single-core capabilities and overall multi-core or system-wide throughput. In stress-ng single-core CPU testing, the instance achieves a top-tier score of 3373.08, placing it in the top 10% of tested hardware. However, because it is limited to a single shared vCPU, its multi-core stress-ng performance remains identical, categorizing it in the bottom 25%. Single-threaded data processing is a relative strength, with gzip compression (31.57 GB/sec) and bzip3 decompression (14.38 GB/sec) both scoring in the top 25%. Conversely, memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is weak at 165.43 GB/sec, though large block read and write bandwidths perform at an average level of 40.43 GB/sec and 36.8 GB/sec, respectively. Complex workloads like Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations register in the bottom 25%, while static web serving throughput is poor at 1.69 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8 alongside a substantial 150 GB NVMe SSD storage allocation on a shared CPU profile. This makes it a resource-dense option for workloads that require moderate memory capacity and fast local storage without demanding continuous, dedicated multi-core CPU performance. The shared CPU allocation introduces potential performance variability, making it less suitable for sustained high-load compute tasks. Recommended workloads include lightweight single-threaded applications, small-scale databases with low transaction volumes, development or staging environments, basic file caching, and low-traffic web serving.
description: Vultr voc-s-1c-8gb-150s-amd is a shared-CPU instance from the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring one AMD EPYC vCPU running at 2.0 GHz, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 150 GB NVMe SSD. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the CPU supports AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng, alongside strong single-threaded gzip and bzip3 processing. However, due to its single shared vCPU, multi-core performance, database operations, and static web serving throughput are weak to poor. The instance offers a high memory-to-core ratio of 1:8, making it a qualitatively cost-effective choice for memory-reliant, low-concurrency tasks. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, development environments, and small-scale databases.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-1c-8gb-150s-amd is a shared AMD EPYC instance with 1 vCPU, 8 GB RAM, and 150 GB NVMe SSD, offering strong single-core performance for lightweight, memory-dense workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-s-1c-8gb-150s-amd features 1 shared AMD EPYC vCPU, 8 GB RAM, and 150 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-threaded processing.
tagline: A shared single-core AMD EPYC instance featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and fast local NVMe SSD storage.

Bundled 150 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong single-threaded compression
High 1:8 memory-to-vCPU ratio for resource-dense configurations
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks, development, and small databases
